title: Level 618
wikitext:
__NOTOC__
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 618: "Industrial Complex"}} {{Class|p1=2|p2=3|p3=1}}
'''Level 618 is the 619th level of the Backrooms'''. {{Blurimage|file=Industrial-urban-street-city-night-scene-with-vintage-factory-warehouses-and-train-tracks.webp|caption=Photo #1}} ==Description== Found hanging by threads over [[The Void]], '''Level 618''', otherwise known as Industrial Complex, is a sprawling compound consisting of various factories and industrial facilities surrounded by a brick wall. Beyond the brick wall lies an expansive parking lot with scrapped machines and rusted vehicles. Each facility is confined by a decrepit wall, which forms a particular grid pattern. Typically, this is observed as a row of factories, each based along a railway and its various offshoots. Near-empty crates and barrels of oil lay scattered around the outsides of buildings and throughout the parking lots. The level remains dark, with faltering yellow streetlights illuminating the roads. Dim stars hang in the sky, though clouds often obscure them. Heavy rain further dulls the level's appearance. The level's consistent point of entry and point of exit make it an invaluable resource. Various facilities are interconnected via railways. Rail lines run between the industrial complex and span farther away into the parking lot. Abandoned carriages and aged engines remain parked on side rails and offshoots. These trains are functional given some maintenance and provide much-needed transportation through the level's various facilities and parking lots. Each facility is separated by roughly 120 kilometre74.5 miless of rail. The facility known to be the consistent entry point is designated Facility 1, and each subsequent facility is given a sequential number. The parking lots may cease suddenly, and the railway can continue across [[The Void]]. It is unknown how the rails remain supported above the Void, only that travel is possible. '''Level 618''' is an area of express interest for both the [[Republic of Aurielle]] and the Republic of Arva/The System due to its industrial capabilities. Many of the factories have fallen under the control of one of the two groups, with eight spaces currently being rendered fully operational and sustainable. Approximately 4 of the said factories produce materials such as various metals and plastics, the origins of which are unknown; the remaining 4 are made for assembling purposes, with Aurielle assembling namely the [[C24N Seborna]], weapons and vehicles, and consumer products. Arva is known to manufacture modified clones of World War II firearms of German and American origin in their respective facilities. Arva has also begun production of the [[Ocelot 1]] main battle tank. There are ongoing efforts to make more facilities fully sustainable. Heavy machinery of varying degrees of functionality can be found inside the facilities, most of which are 70s heavy fabrication machines that are prone to failure, save for eight colonized and modernized facilities. The factories pose the largest threats due to environmental risks: malfunctions, loose roof tiles, and the like. There is a lack of abundant food and safe water, with the issue being addressed by the Republic of Arva in 1985, establishing the Vöckladen supply line that brings them (alongside other materials for manufacturing) into the level. ==Entities== {{Blurimage|file=Gritty-warehouse.webp|caption=Photo #2}} Prior to human colonization in February 1985, the level was infested with entities; however, the Daegen Campaign and the level's subsequent colonization have significantly reduced the native presence. The factories are mostly absent of native life, except for some type of plants that evolved to survive in the level's conditions,known as the Vizschau Anlage however, aside from them, only tamed entities brought from the outside remain. In the parking lot, there have been documented reports collected from Auriellen expeditionary forces that have indicated that [[Entity 8|Hounds]] and [[Entity 3|Smilers]] are still present and also that entity population remains strong beyond colonized quarters, though, similarly to efforts of modernizing and making more facilities sustainable, there are efforts to dwindle their numbers as considerations of further expansion in the levels are made. {{LinkCollapse|initial=After-Action Report |final=Close After-Action Report |body=
[[File:Republic of Aurielle New.png|50px]] [[File:The System Flag.png|50px]]
'''ASSEMBLEU FIUDERALE DI DERENSIO'''

Forward Operating Base no. 7, [[Level 420]]
'''MEMORANDUM FOR'''
TO:ㅤ ㅤㅤGeneral Watch
FROM:ㅤ ㅤFirst Lieutenant Steiger
ㅤ ㅤ ㅤ ㅤ Forward Operating Base no. 7
ㅤ ㅤ ㅤ ㅤ Factories, Level 618
SUBJECT: After Action Report (AAR), Operation Leverage '''INTRODUCTION'''
To cleanse the facilities and peripheral areas of '''Level 618''' to ensure the procurement of Auriellen and System industrial facilities. '''SUMMARY'''
(1) Deployed Location: All compound that incorporate facilities, '''Level 618'''.
(2) Deployed Units: 15th Infantry Company, Group 6. A, Auriellen Security Council Special Forces Operational Detachment—Detail J, Group 9. A.
(3) Duration of Deployment: Began 4th of February, 1985 and ended 10th of April, 1985 (2 Months)
(4) Contingency Planning: Supported by 100 - 150 personnel at Forward Operating Base no. 7 to be deployed if deemed necessary.
(5) Scope of Operation: Ensure a significant decrease or even extinction of entity presence within selected areas of '''Level 618''' to increase the industrial output of both groups involved. '''LOCATION'''
- '''Observation:''' Footholds were established within constant entrances of the level as a temporal operations base. A shortage of material necessities such as food exists in the level. Sizeable demand for logistical support from deployed forces led to some complications due to the aforementioned fact. Forces are currently in the peripheries; non-military personnel have entered and are establishing permanent posts within the level.
- '''Discussion:''' All personnel were restricted to the installation with a kill-on-sight attitude towards any living creature that was not part of the incursion force.
- '''Recommendations:''' In future operations, ensure to provide reconnaissance on a level's resources, environmental dangers, and other facets through exploration teams or drones to accurately provide enough food, material, or other necessary things given the estimated duration of deployment. In addition, with knowledge of the environmental risks, personnel can be briefed regarding these risks, downsizing the possibility of loss of life. '''CONCLUSION'''
We recommend continuing and enhancing security measures to include checkpoints within known entrances of the level and concrete barriers that surround the peripheries of the compound. This is to provide better protection without the necessity of deploying additional security personnel that could be used elsewhere. As seen by concerns, the base requires a better supply chain and, in turn, a permanent dining facility for residents. Finally, we need to upgrade our living and working conditions; tents are adequate for now, but not for the long term considering the temperature the level experiences; thus, more concrete shelters are required. Anything that would improve morale and safety of personnel should be followed, as they would improve the base overall, though proceedings should be made upon review from higher ups. Overall, deployment and colonization are successful.
}} ==Settlements== Aurielle and Arva act as the two dominant powers within Level 618, having colonized it after the Daegen Campaign. During the Lustruger Crescen and initial years of the Korthie Casiagen War, Arva was left the sole governing body due to Auriellen lands coming under temporary Arvan occupation to avoid MEG capture of the strategic location. These lands were returned in late 2002. ===Vallis Descando=== Encompassing terms for all Auriellen settlements within the level, houses a total of 700 residents, most of whom are workers. ===Vöckladen=== The main settlement owned by Arva, it houses 300 individuals. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrance=== *Industrial areas in [[Level 2]] may lead to Level 618; *Some bunkers in [[Level 420]] have tunnels that will lead to Level 618; *Rarely, hallways in [[Level 154]] will lead to Level 618. ===Exits=== *Being in the train long enough will lead you to [[The Void]]; *Elevators in some factories may lead to [[Level 4]]. *Noclipping through a metal wall may lead to [[Level 859]]. ---- {{Footnotes}} ---- {{Collapse|final=
